response.justification: Strong geospatial and navigation domain expertise with proven GIS systems background is valuable, but recent C++ experience is absent—the last substantive work appears dated. Current role focuses on TypeScript/Node.js. While the candidate demonstrates systems thinking (async I/O, microservices at scale, Linux) and strong software craftsmanship practices, they would need to demonstrate C++ fluency quickly or accept significant ramp-up time on the primary technology stack.
strengths: Deep geospatial/navigation domain expertise from multiple projects (Open Green Map, GISCollective, FOSS4G), directly aligned with routing tiling work
Proven systems-level thinking with experience building low-latency, high-throughput backends (microservices handling millions of requests/second, async I/O, real-time metadata)
Strong software engineering discipline across TDD, CI/CD, pairing, and quality assurance practices
concerns: No recent C++ development—last substantial work was LabWindows/CVI components; current role heavily TypeScript/Node.js, creating potential gap in primary job requirement
No Rust experience despite it being offered as an alternative to C++
Limited evidence of distributed systems depth (fault tolerance, consistency models, service discovery) beyond microservices-at-scale experience
